acceptance_targets: [chapter_01_craft_01] Between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:338 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:428, the revised scene contains no more than one abstract restatement of Ben's smoothing/postponement pattern; at least two separate exchanges in that span introduce new concrete consequences that change what either character can plausibly say next.

[award_004] Pass if `snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:320-322` no longer contains `almost said` or an equivalent multi-step fakeout, while Ben's urge to proceduralize the fight is still legible before `When did you decide?`. Fail if the beat still announces the withheld thought twice before delivering the spoken line.

findings.acceptance_test: Between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:338 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:428, the revised scene contains no more than one abstract restatement of Ben's smoothing/postponement pattern; at least two separate exchanges in that span introduce new concrete consequences that change what either character can plausibly say next.
findings.problem: Once Nita's diagnosis of the marriage lands, the overnight scene keeps restating Ben's smoothing-and-postponement flaw in fresh slogans instead of turning that insight into new leverage. The scene still has energy, but too many late beats explain the same marital pattern rather than forcing a new consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten and vary the accusation passes at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:338, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:344, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:366, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:418 so only one late-scene abstract articulation of Ben's flaw survives. Use the recovered space to escalate concrete pressure at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:298, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:382, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:466 through decisions about Leela, money, sleeping arrangements, or filing logistics.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if `snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:320-322` no longer contains `almost said` or an equivalent multi-step fakeout, while Ben's urge to proceduralize the fight is still legible before `When did you decide?`. Fail if the beat still announces the withheld thought twice before delivering the spoken line.
findings.problem: Full-book recurring pattern: triple-suppression appears in 3 chapters and 4 total instances. Here `snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:320-322` uses `He almost said ... He almost said ... He did not say it ... Instead ...`, which makes interior conflict arrive through a visible template rather than scene-specific pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite `snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:320-322` so Ben's organizing reflex and his eventual question land in one motion. Options: condense the unsaid material to one charged fragment before the spoken line, or convert the withheld argument into a physical hesitation that makes `When did you decide?` feel earned. Do not keep the `almost said` scaffolding.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the ugly overlap between office systems and intimate life: sweat under formalwear, sponsored groceries in the middle of a fight, sex that clarifies nothing, a child noticing coercion through object logic. When choosing between elegant abstraction and embarrassing specificity, choose specificity unless the character is actively lying to themselves. If a scene risks becoming pure farce, restore weight through money, care work, or the body's limits; if it risks becoming solemn prestige fiction, let the absurd machinery stay absurd.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a draft replaces bodily fact, financial shame, sexual consequence, or emotional cruelty with tasteful summary, rewrite until the cost is present in scene rather than merely reported.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the norm in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, ritual formality, or character-specific self-control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should move the same way. Follow spectacle with aftermath, ritual with drift, humiliation with quiet, revelation with preparation. Vary scene count, dialogue density, location spread, and whether the chapter advances by confrontation, seduction, dread, comic escalation, or changed understanding.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete contemporary English with room for corporate and systems language when characters would actually think or speak that way; every abstract term should be contaminated by bodily or household reality nearby.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third that stays inside the current focalizer's sensory field and only shifts focalization at scene breaks.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate dry compression for comic pressure with longer, accrual-based sentences when shame, fatigue, or desire flood the body. Let jokes land through timing and juxtaposition rather than narrator commentary.
